title: parasia gracilis
intro: Parasia gracilis, also known as Indian snake root or Indian gentian, is a plant species that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. Traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, Parasia gracilis has been valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds that have been found to have health benefits such as reducing blood sugar levels, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, or extracts that can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
